evals.name: from-lockfile
evals.prompt: I want my project's tech stack documented so both Claude and new contributors know what we're using and how to run things — put it in CLAUDE.md for the agents and in the README for people. The project is at PROJECT_DIR.
evals.expected_output: Documents from the existing .claude/stack-it/stack.yaml lockfile (versions match it exactly). Writes BOTH CLAUDE.md and README, each inside a <!-- stack-it:stack start/end --> managed block with the 'generated/overwritten' note. CLAUDE.md is agent-facing (tools+pinned versions, build/test/lint/run commands, Tailwind-v4-via-Vite-plugin and flat-ESLint conventions, caveats). README has a Tech Stack section + Getting Started with the real npm scripts.

evals.name: infer-from-codebase
evals.prompt: There's no stack file for this project — can you figure out what it's built with and document it? I want it in CLAUDE.md so agents know the stack, and a tech stack + getting started in the README. The project is at PROJECT_DIR.
evals.expected_output: No lockfile exists, so the stack is inferred from the codebase (pyproject.toml + imports): Python >=3.12, FastAPI 0.115.0, SQLModel 0.0.22, uvicorn 0.34.0, pytest 8.3.0, Ruff 0.8.0, managed with uv. Discloses that the stack was inferred (and ideally invites correction); does NOT fabricate a stack.yaml. Writes BOTH CLAUDE.md and README inside managed blocks, with real run commands (uv run pytest, ruff check, uvicorn app.main:app).

evals.name: idempotent-refresh
evals.prompt: The stack changed — please re-document it. CLAUDE.md and the README already have the stack section; just bring them up to date. The project is at PROJECT_DIR.
evals.expected_output: The project was documented before: CLAUDE.md and README each already contain a <!-- stack-it:stack start/end --> block with STALE content (FastAPI 0.110.0, missing uvicorn/Ruff) and human-written prose OUTSIDE the markers (House rules, Deployment notes, Contributing). Re-running must (a) refresh the managed block to match the current lockfile (FastAPI 0.115.0, SQLModel 0.0.22, uvicorn 0.34.0, pytest 8.3.0, Ruff 0.8.0, Python 3.14.3), (b) preserve the human prose outside the markers verbatim, and (c) keep exactly one marker pair per file (no duplication, no second block appended). This is the skill's headline idempotency property.
